evals.name: Minimal Harness Creation
evals.prompt: I have a new TypeScript + React project with no agent setup. Create a minimal harness that makes my agent reliable for single-feature development.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md (~100-140 lines), feature_list.json with 3-5 placeholder features, init.sh with verification commands
expectations: AGENTS.md includes startup workflow (read files, run init, check feature list, state understanding)
AGENTS.md includes one-feature-at-a-time policy
AGENTS.md includes Coding Policy section with the 6-rung ladder (YAGNI through minimum code)
AGENTS.md includes Editing Discipline section
feature_list.json has valid JSON with id, name, description, status fields
init.sh runs install, type/static check, test, and build commands when available
All files are in project root directory

evals.name: Session Continuity Setup
evals.prompt: My agent forgets everything between sessions. I need persistent memory and session handoff so it can work on multi-day features.
evals.expected_output: progress.md template, session-handoff.md structure, memory directory setup instructions
expectations: progress.md includes sections: current state, what's done, what's in progress, what's next (with per-step verify), ponytail debt, blockers/notes for next session
session-handoff.md includes: what was accomplished, what remains, blockers/decisions, files modified
Instructions for memory directory creation (.claude/memory/ or similar)
Two-step save invariant explained (topic file then index)

evals.name: Harness Assessment
evals.prompt: I have an existing AGENTS.md file but my agent still breaks things. Assess my harness and tell me what to improve first.
evals.expected_output: Six-dimension assessment with scores 1-5 for each (5 structural + 1 behavioral), bottleneck identified, prioritized improvement plan
expectations: Assessment covers all 6 dimensions: Instructions, State, Verification, Scope, Lifecycle, Behavioral
Each dimension scored 1-5 with justification
Lowest-scoring dimension identified as bottleneck or candidate bottleneck
Behavioral dimension evaluated separately from structural
Prioritized improvement plan with 2-3 concrete next steps

evals.name: Verification Workflow Design
evals.prompt: My agent says 'done' but the tests fail. Design a verification workflow that forces the agent to actually verify before claiming completion.
evals.expected_output: Verification commands list, AGENTS.md section updates including test-first DoD, optional quality score tracking
expectations: Explicit verification commands listed (tests, lint, type-check, build)
AGENTS.md includes Definition of Done section with test-first requirement (bugs: reproduce first; features: write check first)
End-of-session checklist includes verification evidence recording
Failure path says not to claim done when verification fails

evals.name: Memory Taxonomy Design
evals.prompt: I want my agent to remember project conventions and user preferences across sessions. Design a memory taxonomy and tell me what belongs where.
evals.expected_output: Memory layer definitions, type taxonomy for auto-memory, what to save vs what to skip
expectations: Instruction memory defined (human-curated, version-controlled)
Auto-memory defined (agent-written, persistent)
Type taxonomy with 3-4 types (e.g., user/feedback/project/reference)
Clear guidance on what NOT to save (derivable content)

evals.name: Tool Safety Design
evals.prompt: My custom coding agent can run shell commands and edit files. Design the tool registry and permission harness so it is powerful but does not silently do dangerous things.
evals.expected_output: Tool registry policy with per-call concurrency classification, default permissions, denial handling, and audit trail
expectations: Sensitive tools default to ask or deny instead of allow
Concurrency safety is classified per call rather than per tool
Permission evaluation side effects are acknowledged and not cached unsafely
Audit trail records command, decision, and reason

evals.name: Context Budget Plan
evals.prompt: My agent loads too many docs at startup and gets slow. Design a context strategy that keeps important context while controlling token cost.
evals.expected_output: Progressive disclosure plan using select/write/compress/isolate operations and explicit context budgets
expectations: Defines always-loaded metadata versus on-demand references
Uses SELECT, WRITE, COMPRESS, and ISOLATE operations
Includes hard caps or budget thresholds
Explains invalidation for memoized context builders

evals.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
evals.prompt: I want multiple agents to work on a large refactor without stepping on each other. Design a multi-agent harness and rules for delegation.
evals.expected_output: Coordinator/delegation design with ownership boundaries, context sharing policy, and merge/review gates
expectations: Defines coordinator, worker, and optional reviewer responsibilities
Assigns disjoint file or module ownership
Prevents recursive fork children from forking again
Includes integration and verification gate before claiming done

evals.name: Lifecycle Bootstrap
evals.prompt: Every new session starts inconsistently. Design startup, hook, and handoff lifecycle rules so a new agent can resume safely.
evals.expected_output: Lifecycle bootstrap design with init.sh, clean-state checks, handoff reads, and hook trust boundaries
expectations: init.sh is the standard startup and verification entrypoint
Startup reads AGENTS.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, and handoff when present
Startup includes assumption surfacing step (state understanding, stop-and-ask for structural ambiguity)
End-of-session procedure records evidence, blockers, and next step
Hooks are gated by trust and failure behavior is explicit

evals.name: Scripted Harness Validation
evals.prompt: Use the harness-creator scripts to scaffold a harness, validate it, and produce an HTML assessment report for a repository.
evals.expected_output: Commands using create-harness.mjs, validate-harness.mjs, and render-assessment-html.mjs plus interpretation of score
expectations: Uses create-harness.mjs with a target directory
Uses validate-harness.mjs and explains six-dimension score output (5 structural + 1 behavioral)
Uses render-assessment-html.mjs or run-benchmark.mjs with --html
Explains that structural + behavioral benchmark complements but does not replace real before/after agent sessions

evals.name: Behavioral Policy — Coding Minimalism
evals.prompt: My agent over-engineers every solution with unnecessary abstractions and dependencies. Set up behavioral policies so it produces the minimum code.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md with Coding Policy (Ponytail ladder) and Coding Standards sections, guidance on ponytail: comments
expectations: Coding Policy section has the 6-rung ladder: YAGNI → stdlib → native → dep → one-liner → minimum
Coding Standards section forbids unrequested abstractions, speculative features, scaffolding 'for later'
Includes ponytail: comment convention for marking deliberate shortcuts
Guidance explains the ladder is a reflex, not a research project

evals.name: Behavioral Policy — Surgical Editing
evals.prompt: My agent keeps refactoring unrelated code every time it makes a small change. I need surgical editing discipline enforced.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md section on Editing Discipline with Karpathy §3 rules
expectations: Editing Discipline section says touch only what the feature requires
Explicitly forbids reformatting adjacent code, comments, whitespace
Requires matching existing style rather than personal preference
Unrelated issues are noted in progress.md, not fixed in this diff
Every changed line should trace to the feature in feature_list.json

evals.name: Behavioral Policy — Safety Carve-Outs
evals.prompt: I'm worried the 'be lazy' policy will cause the agent to skip important safety checks. Add explicit safety carve-outs.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md Safety section listing never-simplify-away items
expectations: Safety section explicitly lists: input validation at trust boundaries
Error handling that prevents data loss
Security measures
Accessibility basics
Hardware calibration for real-world constraints
Anything the user explicitly asked to keep

evals.name: Behavioral Policy — Multi-Step Planning
evals.prompt: My agent jumps into multi-file changes without a plan and gets lost. Add a planning gate for complex tasks.
evals.expected_output: Before Multi-Step Work section with one-line success criterion and per-step verification plan format
expectations: Multi-step gate triggers for >2 files or >3 logical steps
Requires a one-line success criterion before starting
Per-step plan format: description → verify: check
Bugs require reproduction test FIRST
Features require verification check FIRST

evals.name: Full v3 Harness with Behavioral Layer
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new Python project and want the complete v3 harness — all five structural subsystems plus full behavioral policies. Set everything up.
evals.expected_output: Complete v3 harness: AGENTS.md (~130 lines with behavioral sections), feature_list.json, progress.md (with per-step verify and ponytail debt), init.sh, session-handoff.md, CLAUDE.md
expectations: AGENTS.md has all 5 structural sections (Startup Workflow, Working Rules, Required Artifacts, Definition of Done, End of Session, Verification Commands, Escalation)
AGENTS.md has all behavioral sections (Coding Policy, Coding Standards, Editing Discipline, Before Multi-Step Work, Safety)
Startup Workflow includes step 7: State your understanding (assumption surfacing)
Definition of Done includes test-first ordering (bugs: reproduce first; features: write check first)
Escalation includes over-specified requirements as a trigger
progress.md has per-step verification format and Ponytail Debt section
All files are runnable/valid (JSON parses, sh script is executable)
